title: Rich Australian Peas Pie
description: A comforting lacto-vegetarian dish featuring frozen peas in a buttery and flaky crust, poached to perfection and served in a banana leaf vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, and pepper.
Add the shortening and use a pastry blender to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the milk, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and roll it out to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Spoon the thawed peas onto one half of the dough, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Brush the edges of the dough with the beaten egg and fold the other half of the dough over the peas to form a triangle or square shape.
Place the pie on a banana leaf and brush the top with the melted butter.
Poach the pie in boiling water for 10 minutes, then bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
Remove the pie from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
